title: Alonso de León

infobox.value: 1639 (1639)  [Cadereyta](/wiki/Cadereyta_Jim%C3%A9nez "Cadereyta Jiménez") (presently Montemorelos), [New Kingdom of León](/wiki/New_Kingdom_of_Le%C3%B3n "New Kingdom of León"), [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain")
21 March 1691(1691-03-21) (aged 51–52)  Cadereyta, New Kingdom of Leon, New Spain

(c. 1639–1691) was an [explorer](/wiki/Explorer "Explorer") and governor in [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain") who led several expeditions into the area that is now northeastern [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") and southern [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas").

outgoing_links.target_title: René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle
Mission San Francisco de la Espada

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Explorer and governor in New Spain

| Alonso de León "El Mozo" | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | 1639 (1639)  [Cadereyta](/wiki/Cadereyta_Jim%C3%A9nez "Cadereyta Jiménez") (presently Montemorelos), [New Kingdom of León](/wiki/New_Kingdom_of_Le%C3%B3n "New Kingdom of León"), [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain") |
| Died | 21 March 1691(1691-03-21) (aged 51–52)  Cadereyta, New Kingdom of Leon, New Spain |
| Occupations | explorer and governor |

**Alonso de León "El Mozo"**  (c. 1639–1691) was an [explorer](/wiki/Explorer "Explorer") and governor in [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain") who led several expeditions into the area that is now northeastern [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") and southern [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alonso_de_Le%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Alonso de León González was born in 1639, in the settlement of [Cadereyta](/wiki/Cadereyta_Jim%C3%A9nez "Cadereyta Jiménez"), [Nuevo León](/wiki/Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n "Nuevo León") in New Spain. He was the third son of General Alonso De León, a celebrated chronicler, historian and [conquistador](/wiki/Conquistador "Conquistador") of the frontier of Nuevo León, and Josefa González.[[1]](#cite_note-cj24-1) To distinguish him from his father, who was also a prominent leader in the colony, sometimes the phrase, *El Mozo,* would later be appended to his name (or its English equivalent, "the younger").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/31/Escudo_Monclova.jpg)](/wiki/File:Escudo_Monclova.jpg)

Alonso de León

De León trained in Spain for a naval career and joined the Spanish navy in 1657. By 1660 he had returned to Nuevo León. He was frequently appointed to lead exploratory parties, and he became an entrepreneur, most notably in [salt mining](/wiki/Salt_mining "Salt mining"). De León married Agustina Cantú and had six children with her.

## Expeditions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alonso_de_Le%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Expeditions")]

In 1684, French explorer [La Salle](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9-Robert_Cavelier,_Sieur_de_La_Salle "René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle") led an ill-fated expedition which was intended to establish a colony at the mouth of the [Mississippi River](/wiki/Mississippi_River "Mississippi River"). Instead, the colonists ended up on the Texas coast, where La Salle established a settlement. When Spanish leaders received word that the French had founded a settlement in the northern portion of their territory, De León was chosen to lead the effort to locate and expel the French colonists.

The first two expeditions, in 1686 and 1687, yielded no evidence of the French colony. One Frenchman, [Jean Gery](/wiki/Jean_Gery "Jean Gery"), was captured during the third expedition in 1688. It was during De León's fourth expedition, in 1689, that he finally discovered La Salle's settlement, which had been abandoned by that time.

## Political offices and later life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alonso_de_Le%C3%B3n&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Political offices and later life")]

De León served as mayor of Cadereyta from 1667 to 1675. He was governor of Nuevo León from 1682 to 1684. In 1687, he was appointed governor of [Nueva Extremadura, New Spain](/wiki/Nueva_Extremadura,_New_Spain "Nueva Extremadura, New Spain").

He was involved in establishing [San Francisco de los Tejas](/wiki/Mission_San_Francisco_de_la_Espada "Mission San Francisco de la Espada") in 1690, the first Spanish mission in East Texas. In doing so, he blazed the trail for much of the [Old San Antonio Road](/wiki/Old_San_Antonio_Road "Old San Antonio Road"). During his expeditions, he named several Texas rivers, including the [San Marcos River](/wiki/San_Marcos_River "San Marcos River"), the [Guadalupe](/wiki/Guadalupe_River_(Texas) "Guadalupe River (Texas)"), the [Medina](/wiki/Medina_River "Medina River"), the [Nueces](/wiki/Nueces_River "Nueces River"), and the [Trinity](/wiki/Trinity_River_(Texas) "Trinity River (Texas)").

De León died in Coahuila on March 21, 1691.
